title: Late fee — first-time offender asking for a waiver
customer_message: Hi, I just noticed a $4.95 late fee on my invoice INV-10234 dated May 1. I paid on May 22 — yes, I missed the due date, but this is the first time it has ever happened in two years on the Pro plan. Can you remove the late fee?
response:
<reply>
I understand your concern about being charged a late fee for the first time since starting with Acme Cloud's Pro plan. Let me quickly review our policies and get back to you shortly. One moment, please.
</reply>

<reasoning>
1. **Classify issue:** This is regarding an incorrect/disputed charge as per customer request for a late fee waiver on their first-time occurrence of being late in payment within the last two years under Pro plan subscription terms, which typically includes 2 months free if billed annually and no refunds after 14 days from monthly plans.
2. **Extract facts:** Invoice date is May 1; charge for a $4.95 late fee on the same day as invoice issuance (May 1); payment made on May 22, which is within the due period but after the policy window of refunds or waivers has passed according to standard policies if applied annually and billed monthly; customer subscribes to Pro plan.
3. **Identify missing facts:** The exact date when annual billing starts/ends for this account, as it affects eligibility for the 2-month free period which could impact late fee waivers or refund policies if applicable within that timeframe.
4. **Apply policy:** As per Acme Cloud's first-time waiver request and considering no other charges are disputed, we can look into this on a case-by-case basis as it is the customer’s first late payment in two years under their Pro plan subscription which could typically include 2 months free if billed annually.
5. **Decide:** Given that there's no explicit policy for waiving fees after they are charged, and since we don't have information about an annual billing cycle starting/ending with the invoice date provided by the customer, I will need to ask one clarifying question before proceeding further.
6. **Sanity-check:** The decision is pending based on additional details regarding their subscription plan timing which could affect eligibility for waivers or refunds if applicable within that timeframe according to standard policies. 
</reasoning>

<reply>
I understand your concern about the late fee charge, and I'm here to help resolve this issue promptly. To proceed with a waiver request as per our first-time policy for waiving fees on initial occurrences of being late in payment within two years under Pro plan subscription terms—which typically includes 2 months free if billed annually—I need one more piece of information from you: When did your annual billing cycle start and end? Could you please provide that so I can accurately assess the situation according to our policies.
</reply>

title: Refund request — inside the 14-day window
customer_message: I signed up for the Starter monthly plan on June 1 and was charged $29. I've barely logged in. Today is June 9 and I'd like to cancel and get a refund please.
response:
<reply>Acknowledged, you would like assistance with your subscription cancellation request for the Starter monthly plan that began on June 1.</reply>  

<reason>According to our policy: "Full refund within 14 days of charge if the service has not been used substantially." Since today is only the eighth day, and considering your minimal usage, you are eligible for a full pro-rated refund.</reason>  

<next step>I will initiate the cancellation process immediately. You should see the $29 credited back to your account within 14 days from today's date as per our policy on no monthly plan charges after this period for a cancelled subscription, unless it was paid annually.</next step>  

<follow-up action>If you have not seen any refund by the end of next week or if there are further concerns regarding your billing issue, please contact us again and we will investigate promptly.

title: Refund request — outside the 14-day window
customer_message: I want a refund for my last month's $99 Pro charge. I was charged on April 1 and I'm only emailing now (May 20) because I forgot. I didn't use the product much last month.
response:
<reply>Thank you for reaching out to us about your concern regarding a potential refund.</reply>

<reason>Based on our policies, we can offer full or pro-rated refunds within 14 days of charge if the service has not been used substantially. However, since it's now past this window and you have requested more than two weeks after invoicing (April 1 to May 20), I will need to check our dispute process for incorrect or late charges.</reason>

<decision>I cannot provide a refund at this time due to the elapsed period since your last payment. However, if you believe there has been an error in billing, we can investigate further within 1 business day as per company policy on disputed/incorrect charges.</decision>

<next_step>Please send us any relevant details or documentation that supports a dispute of the charge so I may begin our investigation immediately. We will update you with findings and next steps by end-of-day tomorrow, May 21st.</next_step>
